comments.body: Get maven to give you the stacktraces for the errors; e.g. mvn -e .... The problem >is< most likely something to do with encoding. Maybe some of the HTML files it is copying are NOT properly encoded UTF-8. Maybe Maven is trying to read them as UTF-8 and that is failing.
For binary files use this solution: stackoverflow.com/a/65933628/14624235

answers.body:
I had this issue and fixed it by adding the below plugin to my pom.xml:

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-resources-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>3.1.0</version>
</plugin>

This is a workaround, and the proper fix is to make sure your files are properly encoded. More details in the selected answer here: Maven clean install: Failed to execute goal org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-resources-plugin:3.2.0:resources

The first response helped me, but it still resulted in a 'Warning'. I resolved it by making the following configuration:

<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-resources-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>3.1.0</version>
    <configuration>
        <encoding>UTF-8</encoding>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

The title brought me here, because the error message was exactly the same, but the issue came from a messages.properties file. For the sake of completion, I'll post the solution here.

The different messages.properties files must be encoded in ISO 8859-1 (see here and here), so if you type in accented letters (for example, àéèöû), it won't be valid in the encoding.

Instead, either use a text editor which handles ISO 8859-1, or use a tool such as this one to encode ISO 8859-1 accents into UTF-8 characters. Here are a few common codes for french accents:

  • é: Ã©
  • è: Ã¨
  • â: Ã¢
  • ê: Ãª
  • ô: Ã´
  • û: Ã»
Settings -> Editor -> File Encodings and switch everything to UTF-8

comments.body: You might prefer exclude by file extension (as describe here : maven.apache.org/plugins/maven-resources-plugin/examples/…) or like @Vlad L shared in his comment above, you can refer to this thread : stackoverflow.com/questions/65910112/…

answers.body:
As a side note, this error may appear if you unintentionally try to filter binary files. (this is not the case of OP, who has HTML files).

In this case, you should create a separate folder for not-filtered resources, and move binary files inside that folder. For example:

    <build>
        ...
        <resources>
        <resource>
            <directory>src/main/resources</directory>
            <filtering>true</filtering>
        </resource>
        <resource>
            <directory>src/main/resources-unfiltered</directory>
            <filtering>false</filtering>
        </resource>
        </resources>
        ...
    </build>

Simple Solution: Ensure application.properties is set to UTF-8

To do this:

  1. Go to Settings → Editor → File Encodings.

  2. Click the '+' and add your application.properties file (steps highlighted in red in the screenshot).

  3. Next, make sure the steps highlighted in blue are correctly checked.

Finally, click Apply to save the changes.

comments.body: Indeed, same error occured during building of my project : I removed all special characters and it solved the issue. Thanks !

answers.body:
I’m not a Maven expert, but I solved this issue easily.

The error happened because I had special characters (like é, è, etc.) in a comment inside my application.properties Maven fails

Solution:

  1. Search through application.properties for any special characters.

2)Replace them with normal characters (like e instead of é)

3)Make sure the file is saved in UTF-8 encoding.

After doing this, Maven builds the project without error. I hope this helps someone encountering the same problem!

title: failed with MalformedInputException: Input length = 1 when building java application with maven 3.9.0
body:
The java application is building perfectly fine with maven 3.6.0-3.8.x . However with maven 3.9.0 it is giving this strange error for html or xml files.

Failed to execute goal org.apache.maven.plugins:maven-resources-plugin:3.3.0:resources (default-resources) on project {project_name}: filtering {path1}/30168019.html to {path2}/30168019.html failed with MalformedInputException: Input length = 1 -> [Help 1]

First I thought might be some encoding issue wrt maven but in the pom file it is mentioned utf-8 itself. Does anyobody have any idea what might be causing this error?
